mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls signal an expanding economy and improving corporate earnings outlook. This positive momentum in the real economy translates to higher equity valuations over the medium term as investors price in stronger future growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery in labor markets is a key focus for investors in 2010.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below a critical threshold indicates a severe contraction in the construction sector, a key driver of economic activity. This weakness spills over into broader consumer and business confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level falls below 1000 (thousands of units, SAAR)
evidence_refs: fact:The collapse in housing was central to the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below the 1000k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and decelerating real GDP growth creates a stagflationary environment. This scenario is toxic for equities as it simultaneously erodes real earnings and forces the central bank to consider tightening policy into a slowdown.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL change > 3.5% AND quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a prolonged period of poor equity performance.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to generate a positive alpha over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production indicates that the supply side of the economy is responding robustly to demand. This is particularly bullish during an early-cycle recovery, as it signals operating leverage and strong profit growth without immediate inflationary pressure.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in INDPRO is positive for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal's predictive power (measured by Rank IC) is not statistically significantly higher in early-cycle regimes (e.g., first 2 years after a recession trough) compared to late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its recent low is a highly reliable real-time indicator of a recession. This signal of widespread economic distress triggers a flight from risky assets like equities, causing negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average is > 0.5pp above its 12-month low
failure_condition: In the 12 months following a trigger event, the S&P 500 total return is positive in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: The level of the Federal Funds rate is a primary input into equity discount rates. A higher policy rate, holding all else constant, increases the rate at which future cash flows are discounted, thus reducing the present value of equities and creating a headwind for prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Standard dividend discount models show an inverse relationship between interest rates and stock prices.
failure_condition: The simple correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive and statistically significant over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real consumer spending drives corporate revenues. When nominal retail sales growth (RSAFS) significantly outpaces inflation (CPIAUCSL), it signals robust real demand, which positively impacts near-term earnings expectations and equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in RSAFS exceeds Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL by at least 2 percentage points.
failure_condition: A signal based on the interaction (real sales growth) has a lower Sharpe ratio than a simpler signal based on nominal RSAFS growth alone over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low unemployment rate and a restrictive federal funds rate indicates a late-cycle economy where the central bank is actively trying to curb growth to fight inflation. This policy stance is a strong headwind for equities, compressing multiples and slowing earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Market peaks in 2000 and 2007 were preceded by such conditions.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is positive during periods when both trigger conditions are met.
